name: note
description: Manage persistent knowledge notes (markdown fragments with YAML frontmatter that survive across conversations). Use this to store API specs, technical decisions, quirks, preferences, and other evergreen information. Do not use this for temporary debugging output, raw code snippets without context, or information that changes frequently. For searching across both notes and sessions, use search instead.

action.description: Note operation to perform: 'add' creates a new note, 'search' finds notes matching a query, 'list' shows notes in a category, 'get' retrieves a specific note by ID, 'update' modifies an existing note, 'append' adds content to an existing note, 'delete' removes a note, 'categories' lists all note categories, 'tags' lists all tags

category.description: Category name (required for 'add', optional for 'search' and 'list')

content.description: Note body content in markdown (required for 'add' and 'update')

id.description: Note ID, a UUID string (required for 'get', 'update', 'append', 'delete')

in.description: Category name to search within (optional for 'search')

tag.description: Single tag to filter by (optional for 'search')

name: session
description: Manage session summaries (structured conversation summaries that preserve context across sessions). Use this to save, list, search, and retrieve past conversation summaries. Do not use this for storing technical knowledge — use note for that. Do not auto-save sessions without user request; use wrap-up commands instead. For searching across both notes and sessions, use search instead.

action.description: Session operation to perform: 'save' creates a new session summary, 'list' shows recent sessions, 'search' finds sessions matching a query, 'get' retrieves a specific session by ID

title.description: Session title (required for 'save'). Use a concise title like 'Fixed auth bug' or 'Implemented user dashboard'.

session_id.description: Session ID, a UUID string (optional for 'save', auto-generated if not provided)

content.description: Session content in markdown format (required for 'save'). Use the session summary template from SKILL.md.

last.description: Number of most recent sessions to return (optional for 'list', defaults to 10)

date.description: Filter sessions by date: 'today' or ISO date like '2024-01-15' (optional for 'list')

date_range.description: Filter sessions by relative date range: '7d', '30d', '90d' (optional for 'list')

id.description: Session ID, a UUID string (required for 'get' action)

name: search
description: Unified full-text search across both notes AND sessions. Use this to find any information stored in claudemem before starting new tasks. Returns results ranked by relevance with snippets. For note-only searches, use note with action='search'. For session-only searches, use session with action='search'.

query.description: Search query text. Use specific keywords rather than vague phrases.

type.description: Filter results by type: 'note' for knowledge fragments, 'session' for conversation summaries (optional, defaults to both)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (optional, defaults to 10)

name: stats
description: Display statistics about claudemem storage including note count, session count, categories, tags, and storage usage in bytes. Use this to understand what information is currently stored. This is a read-only operation that returns summary data.

name: config
description: Manage claudemem configuration settings. Use this to view, set, or delete configuration options like storage paths, auto-save preferences, or display settings. Do not use this for data operations — use note, session, export, or import instead.

action.description: Configuration action: 'set' stores a key-value pair, 'get' retrieves a value by key, 'list' shows all config key-value pairs, 'delete' removes a config key

name: export
description: Export all claudemem data (notes and sessions) as a compressed tar.gz backup archive. Use this for backup purposes before reinstalling or migrating data to another machine. The export includes all markdown files and rebuilds the SQLite FTS5 index on import. For restoring from backup, use import instead.

output_file.description: Output file path for the backup archive (optional). If not provided, defaults to 'claudemem-backup-YYYYMMDD.tar.gz' in the current directory.

name: import
description: Import claudemem data from a backup archive (.tar.gz file created by export). Use this to restore data from a previous backup or migrate data from another machine. Automatically rebuilds the SQLite FTS5 search index after import. Warning: this may duplicate existing notes if the archive contains data already present. For creating backups, use export instead.

archive_file.description: Path to the backup archive file to import (required). Must be a valid .tar.gz file created by the export action.

name: verify
description: Verify database integrity by checking consistency between the SQLite FTS5 search index and the underlying markdown files. Use this to detect data corruption, orphaned index entries, or missing files. Returns a report showing any mismatches found. Does not modify any data. For fixing issues found, use repair instead.

name: repair
description: Repair claudemem database by fixing orphaned entries and rebuilding consistency between the SQLite FTS5 index and markdown files. Use this only after verify reports issues. This action may delete orphaned index entries and rebuild the search index. For checking integrity without making changes, use verify instead.
